requirementCueLines: ADMISSION REQUIREMENTS
GRADUATION REQUIREMENTS
Admission Requirements
Students who meet the following requirements will be considered for admission to the Bachelor of Science in Urban Design:
5 credits of English composition
A minimum of 40 credits
Complete the online application to the Urban Design program

Prospective students meeting the above criteria are invited to complete the application for admission to the Urban Design major. Applicants should prepare a thoughtful, 1-2 page response to the following prompt:

Note: Students must have at least 40 college-level credits to declare Urban Design as a major. Pre-majors are encouraged to take T GIS 311, and meet with the School of Urban Studies advisor to discuss other courses that will prepare them for the program.

Please visit the UW Tacoma Admissions website to submit the appropriate application or to connect with them. You will need to be admitted to UW Tacoma prior to declaring your major. Interested applicants are encouraged to meet with the School of Urban Studies advisor to discuss admissions and degree requirements.
Degree Requirements (beginning Winter Quarter 2026)
Major Requirements:
Shared Curriculum (20 credits)
Urban Design core courses (61 credits)
Shared Curriculum Courses (20 credits)
T URB 101 Exploring Cities (5)
T URB 103 Urban Studies in Practice (3)
T URB 110 Urban Data Analysis (5)
T URB 200 Introduction to Urban Research (3)
T URB 403 Professional Development for Urban Careers (2)
Urban Design Core Courses (61 credits)
T GIS 311 Maps & GIS (6)
T UDE 210 Introduction to Urban Design History and Theory* (5)
T URB 220 Introduction to Urban Planning (5)
T UDE 260 Urban Design Studio I - Introduction to Urban Design* (5)
T UDE 310 Social Production of Space (5)
T UDE 340 Urban Design Studio II (5)
T UDE 350 Urban Design Studio III (5)
T UDE 360 Urban Design Studio IV – Tactical Urbanism (5)
T UDE 440 Urban Design Studio V (5)
T UDE 450 Urban Design Studio VI (Senior Project – Part I) (5)
T UDE 460 Urban Design Studio VII (Senior Project – Part II) (5)
One of the following classes (5 credits):
T URB 250 Immigration, Race, and American Cities (5)
T URB 312 Race & Poverty in Urban America (5)
T URB 322 Land Use Planning (5)
T URB 480 Housing in the United States (5)

The additional credits needed to meet University credit requirements are general electives. Students may focus on an in-depth area of study (e.g., a minor or a certificate) or explore the liberal arts, social sciences, business, social work or health-related fields.
Degree Requirements (for students admitted prior to Winter 2026)
Introductory courses (16 credits)
Urban Design core courses (60 credits)
Major electives (10 credits)

General electives. The additional credits needed to meet University credit requirements are general electives. Students may focus on an in-depth area of study (e.g., a minor or a certificate) or explore the liberal arts, social sciences, business, social work or health-related fields.
Introductory Courses (16 credits)
T UDE 101 Introduction to Computer-Aided Design* (5)
T URB 102 Cities in World Development (5)
T URB 103 Urban Studies in Practice (1)
Urban Design Core Courses (57 credits)
One of the following two classes (5 credits):
T URB 250 Immigration, Race, and American Cities
T URB 312 Race & Poverty in Urban America
Major Electives (10 credits)
Choose at least 2 courses in Sustainable Urban Development (T SUD)

*Students transferring to UW Tacoma from other colleges or universities can satisfy course requirements (listed above and marked with an asterisk) by taking an equivalent course in any of the following areas. 300-400 level studio courses cannot be transferred in.
One Introduction to Computer Graphics or Computer-Aided Design (knowledge of Sketch Up TM is required), and
Graduation Requirements

In order to graduate, in addition to major requirements, students will also be expected to meet general UW university requirements for graduation, many of which can be fulfilled by courses taken to satisfy major requirements. University Graduation Requirements can be found on the Office of the Registrar's website or by running a degree audit .
Degree Requirements
Major GPA: minimum 2.0 in each course required for the major
T UDE 350 Urban Design Studio III
T UDE 360 Urban Design Studio IV
T UDE 440 Urban Design Studio V
T UDE 450 Urban Design Studio VI
T UDE 460 Urban Design Studio VII
Prior to the end of Each Quarter, please complete the below online form to give permission to display your name, picture and urban design project on this website.
